skill.name: image-generator
skill.description: Generates teaching visuals with Gemini browser automation, structured creative briefs, iterative feedback, and a six-gate quality review.
skill.summary: Create and validate professional teaching visuals with Gemini and browser automation.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static high-risk alerts for C2 keywords and weak cryptography are false positives based on the reviewed Markdown context. The skill has legitimate elevated risk because it directs browser automation to Gemini, starts a local browser-use server, downloads assets, and copies files into an application tree.

medium_findings.title: External Browser Automation and Web Service Use
medium_findings.description: The skill directs an agent to open Gemini, paste creative briefs, wait for generation, and download images. This is legitimate for the stated image workflow, but it sends user-provided prompts to an external service and depends on browser automation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs navigation to gemini.google.com and browser actions that submit content and download output. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so it is elevated operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Command and File Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to start a browser-use server with Bash, copy downloaded images into an application directory, embed them in lessons, and maintain checkpoints. These actions can modify local project files and should be run in a trusted workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash startup command and file placement instructions are explicit. No arbitrary command construction or destructive action is shown, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code fences or inline code formatting. No Ruby code or shell backtick execution syntax is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each static location corresponds to Markdown fencing or inline formatting in SKILL.md. The only real command is separately documented as a Bash workflow step, not backtick execution.

low_findings.title: High-Risk Keyword Alerts Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The C2 alert refers to a proficiency level in a table, not command and control behavior. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithm use at the frontmatter description line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 135 uses C2 as a learner proficiency level with max elements and grasp time. Line 3 is Markdown frontmatter for a description field, with no cryptographic operation or weak algorithm invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated External Image Generation Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines external browser navigation, prompt submission, downloads, and local file organization. Users should avoid submitting confidential content and should review downloaded assets before embedding them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow is clearly documented and involves a third-party service plus local file writes. The reviewed file does not show credential access, hidden exfiltration, or malicious prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Educational teams need clear visuals that explain complex ideas without manual design rework. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through Gemini-based generation, quality checks, and lesson embedding.
actual_capabilities: Transforms structured creative briefs into Gemini image generation prompts.
Guides browser automation steps for opening Gemini and selecting image creation.
Applies six quality gates for spelling, layout, color, typography, teaching value, and uniqueness.
Supports iterative feedback when a generated image fails a quality gate.
Organizes approved images into part and chapter paths for lesson use.
Defines batch mode behavior for generating multiple teaching visuals.
limitations: Requires access to Gemini and a working browser-use setup.
Does not generate stock photos or decorative images as its main purpose.
Quality checks depend on visual inspection and may need human review.
External service terms, availability, and output quality can change.

use_cases.title: Create Lesson Illustrations
use_cases.description: Generate chapter images that match a lesson story, concept, and target learner level.

use_cases.title: Build Technical Diagrams
use_cases.description: Convert technical teaching briefs into diagrams with clear hierarchy and color semantics.

use_cases.title: Produce Visual Batches
use_cases.description: Run a structured workflow for many visuals while tracking completed and deferred assets.

prompt_templates.title: Generate One Visual
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single teaching image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-generator skill to create one teaching visual from this brief. Verify all six quality gates before downloading.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Failed Visual
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request after a quality gate fails.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this generated image against the six gates. Identify failed gates and provide principle-based feedback for one Gemini iteration.

prompt_templates.title: Create Chapter Assets
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for several lesson images.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate all visuals for this chapter. Use a new chat for each visual, validate each result, then organize approved assets by chapter.

prompt_templates.title: Resume a Batch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request after an interrupted session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the checkpoint, identify completed and remaining visuals, then resume generation from the first unfinished item.

output_examples.input: A brief for an illustration explaining orchestration in a distributed system.
output_examples.output: A generated teaching visual is reviewed for spelling, layout, color, typography, concept clarity, and uniqueness before download.

output_examples.input: A batch request for sixteen course images across one part.
output: Approved images are saved in the expected part and chapter folders.
Deferred images are listed with the quality issue that blocked approval.
A completion summary records generated and deferred counts.

best_practices: Use full creative briefs with story, intent, metaphor, colors, and typography hierarchy.
Review every generated image against all six gates before saving it.
Use a new Gemini chat for each visual to avoid context contamination.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential course material to external image services.
Do not accept the first generated image without checking every quality gate.
Do not convert reasoning briefs into rigid pixel specifications.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured teaching visuals, diagrams, and chapter illustrations through Gemini browser automation.

faq.question: Does it generate images directly inside Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: No. It guides an agent to use Gemini through browser automation and then review the output.

faq.question: Is this suitable for stock photography?
faq.answer: No. The skill says it is for teaching visuals, not stock photos or decorative images.

faq.question: Why are quality gates included?
faq.answer: They reduce common image generation failures, including spelling errors, weak layout, wrong colors, and unclear teaching value.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: The workflow expects browser-use support, Gemini access, and permission to save generated images into the project.

faq.question: What security precautions should users take?
faq.answer: Users should avoid private data in prompts, review downloads, and run file operations only in trusted workspaces.
